page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a high-capacity instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 64 x86_64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Turin processors running at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The hardware includes 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a generous 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 3840 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong capabilities, achieving a top-tier single-core CPU performance score of 3697.29 in stress-ng, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Its multi-core performance is also robust, scoring 117958.63 in stress-ng, which ranks in the top 25%. However, a key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation model. While this shared configuration reduces overall hosting costs for high-density resources, it may introduce performance variability under sustained, concurrent multi-core workloads compared to dedicated CPU instances.

Given the substantial 512.0 GB memory capacity and 3.8 TB of fast NVMe SSD storage, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, caching layers, and data processing pipelines that require high memory density and local storage capacity without requiring guaranteed, continuous 100% CPU allocation.
description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 x86_64 vCPUs on AMD EPYC-Turin processors running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a bundled 3840 GB local NVMe SSD. In stress-ng benchmarks, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance (top 25%). However, the CPU allocation is shared, presenting a cost-effective tradeoff for workloads that do not require dedicated compute resources. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance with a shared CPU allocation.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage on a shared CPU model.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring sixty-four cores, half a terabyte of RAM, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.

512.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark performance
